mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening demand conditions that depress corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations that increase equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of periods where INDPRO declines sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market dynamics that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening consumer and business demand that depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS and GDPC1 decline sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 110.
